summary: Created soft-delete migration adding deleted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NULL to document_uploads, document_extractions, and document_extraction_items tables. Added an index on document_uploads(deleted_at) for efficient filtering. All ALTER TABLE statements use ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency. Registered db:migrate:soft-delete and db:migrate:soft-delete:dev npm scripts following the existing naming pattern. Added the migration step to both the production deploy command list (after document-groups, before docker compose up -d) and the PR environment migration chain in deploy.yml.
notes_for_reviewer: The migration adds deleted_at to all three document tables but only creates an index on document_uploads. The plan spec only requested the one index on document_uploads — no index on document_extractions or document_extraction_items was specified.

The PR environment migration chain is a single shell string (sh -c '...'), so the new migration was appended with && rather than as a separate quoted array entry — consistent with how that block already works.
No TypeScript compilation was run locally since the file has no custom types beyond the pool import, which matches all other migration files verbatim.
